data.text: I’ve confirmed the package is small and self-contained. Next I’m reading the bridge/store code plus the prior deadcode evidence to decide whether the current functions are production-facing hooks or test/support leftovers.

The code already contains a hidden “keepalive” root in `internal/metric/reachability.go`, so I’m tracing whether the production CLI ever calls it or whether deadcode is still missing the real entry path. If the bridge is already intentionally rooted, the remaining work will likely be to make that rooting explicit and testable rather than invent new runtime behavior.

I found that the CLI already has a dedicated `metric` command path, which means these functions are likely intended to stay live rather than being deleted. I’m now checking whether the current deadcode anchor is sufficient or whether the production graph still misses the bridge through command construction.

The root command already calls `metric.KeepReachabilityForDeadcode()`, so this is not a missing import path. I’m checking whether the metric package is only anchored through an inert helper that deadcode still prunes, which would mean the fix is to move the production wire into a reachable function or to make the anchor a real runtime call site.

input.command: /bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,220p' cli/internal/exec/reachability.go && printf '\\n---\\n' && sed -n '1,220p' cli/internal/persona/reachability.go && printf '\\n---\\n' && sed -n '1,220p' cli/internal/metaprompt/reachability.go"
/bin/zsh -lc "cd cli && go run golang.org/x/tools/cmd/deadcode@v0.42.0 ./... 2>&1 | rg 'internal/metric|dead symbols|unreachable' -n"

data.text: The exec/persona/metaprompt packages use the same pattern, so I’m comparing the metric package against them rather than inventing a new mechanism. If deadcode still reports these symbols after the same style of anchor, I’ll likely need to delete the dead keepalive path and wire the real CLI commands in a way the analyzer can follow.

data.text: I’ve got the final classification now: the old `Init/SaveDefinition/AppendHistory` store lifecycle is already gone, the bridge helpers were renamed, and the remaining store methods are live through the CLI metric subcommands. I’m writing the decisions log with that split so the bead has one auditable line per historical symbol.
The evidence file is in place. I’m running the two required gates plus the repo pre-commit hook now; if anything fails, I’ll fix it before I stage and commit.
